content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Obsoletes GO:0060986 (endocrine hormone secretion) with replaced_by GO:0046879 (hormone secretion), as requested in #17084.

The distinction between endocrine and other modes of hormone dispersal (paracrine, autocrine) is not always clear-cut, and this term was unevenly used relative to its parent. This was discussed extensively since 2019, and the consensus is to flatten the hierarchy.

## Changes

- **Obsoleted GO:0060986** with proper metadata:
  - Name prefixed with "obsolete"
  - Definition prefixed with "OBSOLETE."
  - All relationships removed (is_a, part_of)
  - All synonyms removed
  - `replaced_by: GO:0046879` added
  - `is_obsolete: true` set
  - term_tracker_item and obsoletion comment added

- **Rewired 7 child terms** from `is_a: GO:0060986` to `is_a: GO:0046879`:
  - GO:0032274 gonadotropin secretion
  - GO:0032334 inhibin secretion
  - GO:0035898 parathyroid hormone secretion
  - GO:0035929 steroid hormone secretion
  - GO:0045443 juvenile hormone secretion
  - GO:0051458 corticotropin secretion
  - GO:0070162 adiponectin secretion

## External impact

Per prior research, CL and UBERON have already removed their references to GO:0060986. FBbt lists it in imports but does not use it in axioms.

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed, intent clear, plan created
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Ontology validates before changes (all 20 SPARQL checks pass)
- [N/A] RESEARCH: No literature research needed for straightforward obsoletion
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Relevant terms consulted
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS: Not applicable (obsoletion, not creation)
- [x] EDITS: Checkout/checkin procedure followed
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: Child terms rewired to parent GO:0046879
- [x] METADATA: term_tracker_item, comment, is_obsolete, replaced_by all correct
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passes (all 20 SPARQL checks, 0 violations)
- [N/A] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: No new references introduced
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED: Single commit with detailed message
- [x] ISSUE-ALIGNMENT: Changes match the specific request from @raymond91125

Closes #17084

---

The distinction between endocrine and other modes of hormone dispersal (paracrine, autocrine) is not always clear-cut, and this term was unevenly used relative to its parent. This was discussed extensively since 2019, and the consensus is to flatten the hierarchy.

content.text: Now let me find the comment on GO:0060986 that needs editing.

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0060986
name: obsolete endocrine hormone secretion
namespace: biological_process
def: "OBSOLETE. The regulated release of a hormone into the circulatory system." [GOC:dph]
comment: The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents fake precision. The distinction between endocrine and other modes of hormone dispersal (paracrine, autocrine) is not always clear-cut, and this term was unevenly used relative to its parent. See https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/17084.
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/17084" xsd:anyURI
is_obsolete: true
replaced_by: GO:0046879
created_by: dph
creation_date: 2010-01-11T09:03:48Z
